entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor, the primary crop studied for seed production under heat stress

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: High temperature stress (>36-38oC) occurring during flowering time

entities.name: POLLEN GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of pollen grains germinating, which is reduced by high temperature stress

entities.name: FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The process of seed formation which fails under high temperature stress

entities.name: FLOWER ABORTION
entities.description: The failure of flowers to develop, caused by high temperature stress

entities.name: SAMSORG 45
entities.description: An improved variety of sorghum tested for productivity

entities.name: SAMSORG 46
entities.description: An improved variety of sorghum tested for productivity

entities.name: SAMSORG 41
entities.description: An old but popular variety of sorghum tested for productivity

entities.name: SAMSORG 17
entities.description: An old but popular variety of sorghum tested for productivity

entities.name: SUDAN AND SAHEL ZONES
entities.description: Agricultural zones in Nigeria where sorghum varieties were released

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water to crops to assess productivity during the dry season

entities.name: DADINKOWA
entities.description: The specific location in Northern Nigeria where the experiment was conducted

entities.name: DRY SEASON
entities.description: The period of the year characterized by low rainfall when irrigation is used

entities.name: PLANTING DATE
entities.description: The specific timing of sowing (October and January) to assess seed production

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The ability of the crop to produce viable seeds, affected by temperature and planting date

entities.name: STERILE PANICLE
entities.description: Panicles that fail to set seeds due to high temperature during flowering
